about.description:
A graphic designer based in Den Haag, NL.
Often playing between poetics and design, drawing from personal experiences of nostalgia and memory. Taking inspiration from nature, and choosing listening to the world as a research method. I often work with writing poetry or stories, installation, sound, and publications.

projects.title: a conversation with my mother
projects.description: Using catalogues from the best dutch book design, from the year of my mother and my own birth. Transcribing our conversation from Swiss-German to English.

projects.title: plantasia with Rilke
projects.description: This publication plays with using the riso printer and scanner, experimenting with only using the printer in it’s analogue format. Using texts from the poet Rainer Maria Rilke, and flowers found.

projects.title: shorthand Poetry
projects.description: An experiment using Gregs Shorthand to create visual poetry, combining it with photography.

projects.title: i'm busy with my to do lists
projects.description: Poetry reading with live sound play, in collaboration with Julie Laures. Working from the theme of over-production and to do lists piling up, with a research publication and also live text printing.

projects.title: storytelling
projects.description: A collection of stories over the years

projects.title: transcriptions of women in print
projects.description: Working from an interview held between Suzanne Snider and Elana Dykewomon, this publication focuses on the transcriptions that keep the oral history of women in print alive.

projects.title: gifting communication
projects.description: Beginning from a story written about a messenger between the ocean and the forest. Using text I’ve collected – as well as written to illustrate the action of gift giving as a form of communication.

projects.title: thirty o'four twenty six
projects.description: A live performance with Julie Laures. Inspired by a dying worm on the Amsterdam metro in september.

projects.title: xs zine
projects.description: A process book turned into a zine, a record of the events and conversations happening during a collective session of transcribing.

projects.title: dinner as a research method
projects.description: In collaboration with Maja Usak, Anna Zeller, Lisa Oort and Theodora Fischer. Exploring having dinners as a method of research, helping to guide decisions on what objects must be created for a dinner to take place. And what comes out of having dinners within a social setting.

projects.title: metallic ornamentation
projects.description: Typographic ornaments inspired by the metal structures found in the city of Basel, Switzerland.

projects.title: ritual of scent
projects.description: Inspired by the topic of metamorphosis, this scent activated installation serves as a reminder of the places we have been and the experiences we have created. The rituals we perform have always been an important part of our human experience, since the beginning of life. This work asks the question, what smells and symbology represent life and death? And how can we find the balance fine of this within the cycle of metamorphosis.

projects.title: the dragonfly
projects.description: Paper sculpture, home to a lone dragonfly.

projects.title: stta
projects.description: Together with Maja Usak. A quick poster for ‘Something to think about’.

projects.title: nausicaä
projects.description: Stemming from the visions of Nausicaa from studio ghibli, and the text from Braiding Sweetgrass by Robin Wall Kimmerer. Using my own writing and collage as the binder, I explore the importance of perserving nature.

projects.title: the little bow
projects.description: Typeface created with a single ribbon, the form being influenced by the alphabet itself. A play on taking tradition away from typography.

projects.title: shadows of symbols
projects.description: A publication for a philosophy essay.

wormhole.title: Like washed up starfish
wormhole.content: Like washed up starfish, walking over their bodies. Even magpies singing are not the same.The sea that brings in icy air, And doesn’t hum to me gently         In my sleep. The currency of the ocean is that of a new, Forced into my open palm. Each fragile bone I crush as I walk, reflects within my own warm casket. Painfully bare and cold. I build my house here, with windows facing south. One kind object at a time, I shall rest my body.                      Letting my styrofoam heart bleed.

wormhole.title: Silent walk
wormhole.content: Bats in silence.                               Hidden within the trees are hanging bats of rotten leaves.         Weighing above me, the light of thorns of webs.       The buzzing of caught insects, humming in the air.         The breaths of my ancestors calling my name.
